wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Fluentoria project overview section. Explain the purpose of this immersive language learning platform, its core value proposition as a gamified LMS, and key features including habit-based learning, progress tracking, and achievement systems. Document the target audience (language learners and educators), primary use cases, and how the platform differs from traditional language learning approaches. Include the technology stack overview, architectural philosophy, and integration points with Firebase, Asaas, and YouTube. Provide both conceptual overviews for stakeholders and technical highlights for developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and include practical examples of common user workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Flows
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for authentication flows covering email/password authentication, Google OAuth integration, and social login mechanisms. Document the complete authentication lifecycle from user input through Firebase Authentication APIs. Explain form validation, error handling strategies, loading states, and user feedback mechanisms. Include code examples showing signInWithEmailAndPassword, createUserWithEmailAndPassword, and signInWithPopup implementations. Detail the authentication state management, session persistence, and automatic sign-in flows. Address common authentication errors, their causes, and resolution strategies. Provide practical examples of form handling, input validation, and real-time authentication status updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Course Creation & Editing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the course creation and editing functionality. Document the CourseForm component architecture, form validation, and data submission workflows. Explain the course creation process including title, description, author, duration, and content type selection. Detail the editing capabilities for existing courses, including media uploads, cover image management, and content updates. Include practical examples of form handling, file upload processes, and database operations. Document the relationship between course metadata and content delivery, including video URL integration, audio file management, and PDF resource handling. Address form validation rules, error handling, and user feedback mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive frontend architecture documentation for the Fluentoria React application. Describe the component hierarchy starting from App.tsx as the root component, including the screen-based routing system with lazy loading and code-splitting. Document the state management architecture using Zustand stores (appStore, courseStore) for global state coordination. Explain the authentication flow integration with Firebase, user role management, and access control patterns. Detail the responsive design implementation with mobile navigation, sidebar layout, and PWA functionality. Include component composition patterns, error boundary integration, and suspense-based loading states. Address performance optimizations including lazy loading strategies, memory management, and concurrent rendering benefits from React 19.2.0.

wiki_catalogs.name: Achievement System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the achievement system. Explain the achievement data model including achievement types (course_count, streak_days, hours_studied, first_course), unlock criteria, and XP reward mechanisms. Document the achievement configuration system, progress calculation algorithms, and unlock conditions. Detail the UI components for displaying unlocked vs locked achievements, progress bars for partial completion, and achievement icons. Include examples of achievement definitions, conditional logic for unlocking, and integration with student progress tracking. Explain the relationship between learning milestones and achievement triggers, including behavioral psychology principles applied in the system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Video Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the video integration system. Document the YouTube API integration including video ID extraction, embed URL generation, and iframe configuration. Explain the support for Google Drive videos and direct video URLs (MP4, WebM, OGG). Detail the video player implementation with React refs, duration detection, and metadata handling. Include technical specifications for video format support, quality settings, and streaming optimization. Document the fallback mechanisms for different video sources and error handling strategies. Provide examples of video URL validation, thumbnail generation, and responsive video player sizing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Asaas Payment Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Asaas payment integration component. Document the payment processing workflow including customer creation, credit card validation, and transaction processing. Explain the React component implementation with form validation, input formatting, and step-by-step payment flow. Detail the integration with Asaas API including authentication, request/response handling, and error management. Include security considerations for PCI compliance, data encryption, and tokenization. Provide examples of payment scenarios, failure handling, and retry mechanisms. Document the component's props interface, state management, and integration with Firebase authentication.

wiki_catalogs.name: Firestore Schema Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the Firestore database schema design. Detail all entity relationships including Users, Courses, Lessons, Modules, StudentCompletions, Achievements, and Media collections. Document field definitions, data types, validation rules, and required/optional properties for each collection. Explain the hierarchical structure with parent-child relationships and embedded documents. Include primary keys, foreign key references, and indexing strategies. Provide practical examples of document structures, sample data entries, and common query patterns. Address data normalization vs denormalization decisions and performance implications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Dashboard Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the admin dashboard overview and navigation system. Explain the centralized admin interface design, navigation components including sidebar and mobile navigation. Document the dashboard layout, tab-based content organization, and admin-specific UI patterns. Detail the integration between navigation components and routing, admin role permissions, and dashboard accessibility features. Include practical examples of dashboard navigation workflows, responsive design considerations, and admin interface customization options.

wiki_catalogs.name: Button Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Button component system. Detail all button variants (primary, secondary, ghost, destructive, outline), sizes (sm, md, lg, icon), and states (loading, disabled). Document the component's props interface, forwardRef implementation, and Tailwind CSS class combinations. Include practical usage examples showing different button configurations, accessibility considerations, and integration patterns. Explain the design tokens used (btn-primary-pluma, btn-ghost-pluma), animation effects during transitions, and responsive behavior. Provide guidelines for button composition, custom styling approaches, and best practices for consistent button usage across the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Firebase Cloud Functions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for Firebase Cloud Functions. Document the Asaas webhook endpoint (/asaasWebhook) with HTTP methods, request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and webhook verification process. Detail the updateUserCustomerId callable function with parameter validation and error handling. Document the runAccessMigration callable function for administrative data migration with admin context validation and Bearer token authentication fallback. Include request/response examples, error codes, security considerations, rate limiting, and integration patterns. Address webhook signature verification, timing-safe comparison, and CORS configuration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Hierarchy & Composition
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive component hierarchy documentation for the Fluentoria React application. Detail the root App.tsx component structure and its role as the central orchestrator for authentication state, screen routing, and user role management. Document the component composition patterns including the sidebar navigation system, mobile navigation adaptation, and authentication wrapper components. Explain the conditional rendering logic for different user roles (student vs admin), screen-based routing with lazy loading, and component lifecycle management. Include the relationship between parent and child components, prop drilling patterns, and how the component tree adapts to different user states and screen modes. Address the integration of UI components, error boundaries, and suspense loading states within the overall component architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Firebase Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Firebase integration in Fluentoria. Detail the Firebase initialization process including authentication, Firestore database, Cloud Storage, and Cloud Functions setup. Explain the security rules implementation for Firestore and Cloud Storage, covering read/write permissions, user authentication requirements, and data validation rules. Document the real-time database synchronization patterns, offline persistence configuration, and multi-tab cache management. Include environment variable configuration, deployment settings, and Firebase project structure. Address authentication token management, user session handling, and role-based access control implementation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Create Customer Function
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the create-asaas-customer Netlify function. Document the customer creation workflow, including data validation, Asaas API integration, and response handling. Explain the function's parameters, request schema, and success/error responses. Detail the integration with Firebase authentication for authorization and the customer data structure. Include examples of successful customer creation, error scenarios, and debugging approaches. Document security measures, rate limiting, and monitoring considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Check Payment Status Function
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the check-payment-status function. Document the POST endpoint that validates Firebase tokens and checks Asaas payment status for customers. Detail the authentication flow using JWT verification with Google's JWK set, request parameters including customerId, and response schemas with authorization status, payment status (no_payment, active, overdue), and payment history. Include error handling for missing tokens, invalid tokens, configuration errors, and Asaas API failures. Provide examples of successful payment validation scenarios and common error responses.

wiki_catalogs.name: Asaas Webhook Endpoint
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Asaas webhook endpoint (/asaasWebhook). Document the HTTP POST endpoint with mandatory webhook token verification using crypto.timingSafeEqual for security. Detail the webhook payload structure including event types (PAYMENT_RECEIVED, PAYMENT_CONFIRMED, PAYMENT_OVERDUE), payment data, and customer information. Explain the automatic user creation and update logic, course mapping via externalReference parsing, and multi-product support. Document the CORS preflight handling, error responses (401 Unauthorized, 400 Bad Request, 500 Internal Server Error), and webhook signature validation process. Include request/response examples, timing-safe comparison implementation, and integration patterns with Asaas payment system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Firestore Collections
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Firestore collection schemas and data models used in Fluentoria. Document the structure and field definitions for all major collections including users, courses, user_courses, mindful_flow, music, completions, and students. Detail data types, validation rules, and relationships between collections. Include examples of document structures, field constraints, and indexing requirements. Explain collection organization patterns, data normalization strategies, and referential integrity considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for Fluentoria development. Include detailed prerequisites (Node.js, pnpm, Firebase CLI), step-by-step installation instructions, environment setup with environment variables, and initial project configuration. Document the development workflow including running the development server, building for production, and testing procedures. Explain the project structure, key configuration files, and common development commands. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues and environment-specific considerations. Include practical examples of first-time setup and verification steps to ensure proper installation.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Roles & Permissions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the role-based access control system implementing Student and Admin user types. Detail the user role hierarchy, permission matrices, and access control mechanisms throughout the application. Document how user roles are determined, stored, and enforced in Firestore collections. Explain the admin verification process, role assignment workflows, and permission inheritance patterns. Include practical examples of conditional rendering based on user roles, route protection strategies, and feature gating mechanisms. Address role-based UI components, administrative controls, and user management interfaces. Document the security implications of role-based access and best practices for maintaining access control integrity.

wiki_catalogs.name: Course Catalog & Organization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the course catalog system and organization features. Detail the CourseList component implementation, including course display logic, thumbnail generation, and progress tracking visualization. Document the search and filtering capabilities, including keyword search by title and author, category filtering, and progress-based sorting. Explain the responsive layout design with course cards, sticky sidebar behavior, and mobile navigation considerations. Include examples of course data fetching, real-time updates, and user-specific course filtering. Document the integration with YouTube thumbnails, cover image fallbacks, and progress bar rendering. Address performance optimizations for large course catalogs and lazy loading strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Services Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create backend services architecture documentation for Fluentoria's serverless infrastructure. Document the Firebase integration pattern including Authentication, Firestore database, and Cloud Functions. Explain the dual backend approach combining Firebase Cloud Functions and Netlify Functions for payment processing. Detail the Asaas payment integration workflow, customer management, and payment status tracking. Include Firebase security rules implementation, real-time database synchronization, and authentication token management. Address serverless function deployment, environment variable configuration, and error handling strategies. Document the API gateway patterns and how frontend components interact with backend services.

wiki_catalogs.name: XP Reward Mechanics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the XP reward system. Explain XP calculation algorithms, reward multipliers, and activity-based XP generation. Document the XP accumulation process, level progression requirements, and XP distribution across different learning activities. Detail the level-up system including experience thresholds, level bonuses, and progression milestones. Include examples of XP calculations for various activities, streak bonuses, and achievement-based XP rewards. Explain the mathematical formulas used for XP scaling, exponential growth patterns, and leveling mechanics. Address XP decay systems if implemented and strategies for maintaining player engagement through balanced XP economics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Audio Material Playback
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the audio material playback system. Detail the audio file format support (MP3, WAV, FLAC), browser compatibility, and native HTML5 audio element usage. Document the audio player interface including play/pause controls, progress bars, volume adjustment, and timeline navigation. Explain the integration with course lessons for audio materials, including automatic duration detection and progress tracking. Include accessibility features such as keyboard navigation, screen reader support, and captions. Address audio quality optimization, streaming performance, and mobile device considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Netlify Functions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Netlify serverless functions that handle payment processing. Document each function's purpose, parameters, request/response schemas, and error handling. Detail the create-asaas-customer function for customer registration, process-asaas-payment for transaction processing, and check-payment-status for webhook handling. Explain the integration with Asaas API, authentication mechanisms, and security considerations. Include examples of function invocation, environment variable configuration, and debugging strategies. Document the function deployment process, monitoring, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Rules Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Firestore security rules and Cloud Storage access control. Explain the rule syntax, conditional logic, and permission patterns for different user roles (student, admin, primary admin). Document read/write permissions for each collection, field-level security, and data validation rules. Include examples of authenticated access, role-based restrictions, and content moderation controls. Address common security patterns like user ownership verification, admin privileges escalation, and data isolation between users. Provide troubleshooting guidance for permission denied errors and testing strategies for security rules.

wiki_catalogs.name: Student Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the student management system. Explain student enrollment tracking, progress monitoring capabilities, and user administration workflows. Document the student listing interface, enrollment status management, and progress analytics. Detail the integration with Firebase authentication for student accounts, progress data synchronization, and student activity tracking. Include practical examples of student onboarding processes, progress assessment workflows, and administrative student management procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Form Input Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for form input components including standard Input, AnimatedInput, and AI-powered input components. Document prop interfaces, validation patterns, and state management for each input type. Explain the animated input's transition effects, AI input's autocomplete functionality, and standard input's styling variants. Include usage examples for different input types (text, password, email, number), form integration patterns, and accessibility features. Detail the component's integration with form libraries, error handling, and real-time validation approaches. Provide guidelines for input customization, styling consistency, and responsive design patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Netlify Functions API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for Netlify serverless functions used for payment processing. Document the check-payment-status function with request parameters, response schemas, and payment status validation. Detail the create-asaas-customer function for customer creation workflows with Asaas integration. Document the process-asaas-payment function for payment processing with webhook handling and error management. Include HTTP methods, request/response formats, authentication requirements, and integration with Asaas payment system. Provide examples of payment flow orchestration and error handling strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed state management documentation for the Fluentoria application using Zustand stores. Explain the dual-store architecture with appStore for global application state (user authentication, navigation, view modes) and courseStore for course-related state management (selected courses, galleries, modules). Document the store initialization patterns, state update mechanisms, and subscription handling for reactive updates. Detail the custom hooks useCatalogData and useCatalogFilters for data fetching and filtering operations. Include state persistence strategies, performance optimization techniques, and best practices for avoiding unnecessary re-renders. Address the integration between local component state and global store state, including state hydration during authentication and user role changes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Cloud Functions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for Firebase Cloud Functions implementation in Fluentoria. Document the function structure, deployment configuration, and API endpoints including updateUserCustomerId functionality. Explain the serverless architecture patterns, function triggers, and event-driven programming models. Detail the function deployment process, environment variable management, and error handling strategies. Include function scaling, cold start optimization, and monitoring approaches. Address security considerations, authentication requirements for functions, and integration with Firebase services. Document the development workflow, local testing strategies, and debugging techniques for Cloud Functions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Process Payment Function
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the process-asaas-payment Netlify function. Document the payment processing workflow, including credit card validation, transaction execution, and payment status management. Explain the function's integration with Asaas payment API, error handling strategies, and response formatting. Detail the security measures for handling sensitive payment data and PCI compliance requirements. Include examples of payment processing scenarios, failure cases, and webhook integration. Document the function's configuration, monitoring, and troubleshooting procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Create Asaas Customer Function
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the create-asaas-customer function. Document the customer creation workflow with Asaas payment system integration. Detail request parameters for customer data, response schemas for customer creation confirmation, and error handling for duplicate customers, invalid data, and Asaas API failures. Include examples of customer creation flows, required fields validation, and integration patterns with Firebase user authentication. Document the relationship between Firebase user accounts and Asaas customer records.

wiki_catalogs.name: Callable Functions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for Firebase callable functions. Document the updateUserCustomerId callable function with parameter validation, error handling, and Firestore integration. Detail the runAccessMigration callable function with admin context validation, Bearer token authentication fallback, and data migration capabilities. Explain the ensureAdminContext and ensureAdminFromRequest helper functions, legacy migration process for user_courses, mindful_flow, and music collections. Document function invocation patterns, error codes, security considerations, and integration with Firebase Authentication. Include parameter schemas, return value structures, and practical usage examples for both authenticated and unauthenticated scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Query Patterns & Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for Firestore query patterns and data access operations. Document CRUD operations for each collection with examples of read, write, update, and delete operations. Explain filtering strategies, pagination patterns, and complex query construction. Include examples of batch operations, transaction usage, and real-time subscriptions. Address query optimization techniques, performance considerations, and best practices for different data access scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Fluentoria system architecture. Describe the high-level design patterns including React 19.2.0 with concurrent rendering, Zustand-based state management, and Firebase integration. Document the component hierarchy, data flow architecture, and integration patterns between frontend, Firebase services, and external APIs. Explain technical decisions around lazy loading, code-splitting, and performance optimization. Include system context diagrams showing relationships between Firebase Authentication, Firestore, Cloud Functions, Netlify Functions, and Asaas payment processing. Address cross-cutting concerns like authentication, authorization, real-time updates, and error handling. Document the technology stack, dependency management, and deployment topology.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Data Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for user data management including user profile creation, data synchronization between Firebase Authentication and Firestore, and real-time user data updates. Document the createOrUpdateUser function implementation, user document structure in Firestore, and data consistency strategies. Explain user metadata management, profile picture handling, and display name synchronization. Include practical examples of user data CRUD operations, real-time listeners for user updates, and data validation patterns. Address user data privacy considerations, GDPR compliance aspects, and data retention policies. Document the integration between authentication tokens and Firestore user documents, including automatic user creation and profile updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Delivery & Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the course content delivery and management system. Document the CourseDetail component architecture, including lesson navigation, content playback controls, and progress tracking integration. Explain the support for different content types: video lessons with YouTube integration, audio materials with playback controls, and PDF resources with viewing capabilities. Detail the media management system, including file upload handling, storage integration, and content moderation workflows. Include examples of content rendering, user interaction patterns, and progress synchronization. Document the integration with external APIs for video content, audio processing, and document management. Address accessibility considerations, responsive design patterns, and performance optimization for media-heavy content.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create data flow architecture documentation for Fluentoria's information flow patterns. Document the unidirectional data flow from Firebase Firestore through custom hooks to React components. Explain the store-based state management pattern using Zustand, including global state coordination and component-local state. Detail the real-time data synchronization mechanisms, query optimization strategies, and caching patterns. Include the data transformation pipeline from Firestore documents to component props, error handling in data fetching, and optimistic updates. Address performance considerations like pagination, filtering, and batch operations. Document the relationship between database queries, store updates, and UI re-rendering patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Leaderboard System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the leaderboard system. Explain real-time ranking algorithms, score calculation methods, and position determination logic. Document the leaderboard data structure, sorting criteria (XP total, levels completed, streaks), and ranking refresh mechanisms. Detail the podium display system for top performers, full leaderboard table implementation, and user identification features. Include examples of leaderboard queries, pagination strategies, and performance optimization for large datasets. Explain social comparison features, ranking notifications, and integration with achievement systems. Address leaderboard privacy settings, seasonal resets, and competitive gaming elements.

wiki_catalogs.name: PDF Resource Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the PDF resource management system. Document the PDF file handling including upload, storage, and retrieval processes. Explain the embedded PDF viewer implementation with zoom controls, page navigation, and print functionality. Detail the file size limitations, compression strategies, and security considerations for PDF content. Include the support material integration showing how PDFs are displayed alongside video lessons. Document the download functionality, file metadata handling, and preview generation for PDF thumbnails. Address accessibility compliance for PDF viewing and navigation assistance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Firebase Functions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Firebase Cloud Functions used in the payment system. Document the updateUserCustomerId function that synchronizes customer IDs between Firebase Authentication and Asaas. Explain the function's trigger conditions, data validation, and error handling mechanisms. Detail the integration with Firebase Authentication, Firestore database operations, and Asaas customer management. Include examples of function invocation, parameter validation, and response handling. Document the function deployment process, security considerations, and monitoring approaches. Address common issues and troubleshooting strategies for customer ID synchronization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Access Patterns & Queries
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for database query optimization and data access patterns. Document common CRUD operations, batch operations, and transaction patterns used throughout the application. Explain query optimization strategies including indexing, pagination, and filtering techniques. Include practical examples of complex queries, subcollections access, and real-time subscriptions. Address performance considerations for large datasets, caching strategies, and data loading patterns. Document error handling for database operations, retry mechanisms, and graceful degradation strategies. Provide guidelines for efficient data fetching and state synchronization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Course Administration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the course administration system. Explain the course catalog management interface, content creation workflows, and media management capabilities. Document the course form functionality, content filtering and search, and bulk operations. Detail the integration between course data management and Firebase Firestore, content validation processes, and media upload handling. Include practical examples of course creation workflows, content editing procedures, and administrative content management tasks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layout & Container Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for layout and container components including Card, PageHeader, and SettingSection. Detail the structural patterns, spacing systems, and responsive behavior of each component. Document the card component's elevation, padding, and content organization patterns. Explain the page header's typography hierarchy, breadcrumb integration, and action button placement. Cover the setting section's accordion behavior, content organization, and form integration patterns. Include usage examples for different layout scenarios, responsive breakpoints, and accessibility considerations. Provide guidelines for component composition, spacing consistency, and design system integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Query API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Firestore database query interfaces and data access patterns. Document collection schemas for users, courses, user_courses, mindful_flow, music, and other entities with field definitions and data types. Detail query patterns for course catalog filtering, user progress tracking, and content management. Document Firestore security rules implementation, access control mechanisms, and data validation patterns. Include examples of CRUD operations, complex queries, and data aggregation patterns. Address database indexing strategies, performance optimization, and data consistency patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Routing & Navigation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed routing and navigation documentation for the Fluentoria application. Document the screen-based routing system defined in the Screen type, including all available screens (dashboard, courses, gallery, module-selection, course-detail, mindful, music, profile, achievements, leaderboard, attendance). Explain the navigation patterns using the navigateTo function, route guards for authentication and access control, and the integration with lazy loading for performance optimization. Detail the responsive navigation system with desktop sidebar navigation and mobile navigation adaptation. Include the PWA shortcut handling, URL parameter processing, and navigation state management. Address the view mode switching between student and admin modes, and how navigation adapts to different user roles and access levels.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payment Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the payment processing system integrating Asaas payment provider. Detail the three-tier payment workflow: customer creation, payment processing, and status checking functions. Document the webhook integration patterns, payment status synchronization, and subscription management. Explain the Netlify Functions deployment, environment variable configuration, and API endpoint specifications. Include error handling strategies, retry mechanisms, and payment failure scenarios. Address security considerations for payment data, PCI compliance requirements, and data encryption. Document the integration with Firebase for user data updates and access control based on payment status.

wiki_catalogs.name: Check Payment Status Function
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the check-payment-status Netlify function. Document the payment status checking mechanism, webhook processing, and real-time payment updates. Explain the function's integration with Asaas webhooks, payment status verification, and user access control updates. Detail the data synchronization process, error handling for failed checks, and retry mechanisms. Include examples of webhook processing, status update scenarios, and integration with the frontend payment component. Document monitoring, logging, and debugging approaches for payment status tracking.

wiki_catalogs.name: Process Asaas Payment Function
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the process-asaas-payment function. Document the payment processing workflow including payment creation, webhook handling, and status updates. Detail request parameters for payment data, response schemas for payment confirmation, and webhook payload formats for Asaas payment events. Include error handling for payment failures, webhook validation, and integration with Firebase user access control. Provide examples of payment flow orchestration, webhook verification processes, and payment status synchronization with the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: HTTP Migration Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for HTTP migration endpoints with CORS support. Document the runAccessMigrationHttp endpoint with explicit Bearer token authentication, preflight request handling, and manual admin verification. Detail the CORS configuration allowing POST requests with Content-Type and Authorization headers. Explain the migration process for legacy data including user_courses creation, mindful_flow productId updates, and music productId updates. Document error handling strategies, status code responses, and fallback authentication mechanisms. Include request/response examples, CORS preflight handling, and integration patterns for administrative data migration tasks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Rules & Access Control
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Firestore security rules and access control mechanisms. Document permission models for different user roles (student, admin, primary admin) and their respective access patterns. Explain rule syntax, conditional logic, and data validation patterns. Include examples of document-level and collection-level security rules. Address storage security rules for media files, upload permissions, and access restrictions. Document best practices for rule maintenance and debugging security issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & User Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the authentication and user management system. Detail the Firebase Authentication integration including email/password and Google OAuth flows, user registration processes, and session management. Document the role-based access control system with Student and Admin user types, permission hierarchies, and access control mechanisms. Explain user profile management, data synchronization between Firebase Authentication and Firestore, and real-time user status updates. Include security rules implementation, password reset procedures, and account verification workflows. Provide practical examples of authentication guards, protected routes, and user state management throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Rules & Access Control
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Firestore security rules and access control mechanisms protecting user data and system resources. Detail the rule structure for user collections, course data, and administrative functions. Explain authentication-based access control, role-based data filtering, and data validation rules. Document the security rule syntax, conditional logic, and wildcard patterns used throughout the application. Include practical examples of read/write permissions, data masking strategies, and privilege escalation prevention. Address common security vulnerabilities and their mitigation strategies. Document testing approaches for security rules, debugging techniques, and best practices for maintaining secure access control policies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Student Enrollment & Access Control
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for student enrollment and access control mechanisms. Document the enrollment workflow, including course registration, payment verification, and access permission management. Explain the integration between course availability, student progress tracking, and access control based on payment status. Detail the student dashboard functionality, including enrolled course management, progress monitoring, and course navigation. Include examples of enrollment validation, access restriction logic, and user role-based permissions. Document the relationship between enrollment records, course completion tracking, and achievement unlocking. Address security considerations, data validation, and real-time access updates based on enrollment status.

wiki_catalogs.name: Integration Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create integration patterns documentation for Fluentoria's third-party service integrations. Document the Firebase ecosystem integration including Authentication, Firestore, Cloud Functions, and Storage services. Explain the Asaas payment processing integration with webhook handling and status synchronization. Detail the YouTube video integration patterns, media upload workflows, and content delivery optimization. Include the mindful flow and music integration patterns, achievement system integration with gamification APIs, and attendance tracking with external calendar services. Address error handling strategies for external API calls, retry mechanisms, and fallback patterns. Document the configuration management for different environments and service credentials.

wiki_catalogs.name: Streak Tracking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the streak tracking system. Explain daily attendance monitoring, streak calculation algorithms, and habit formation tracking. Document streak preservation mechanisms during breaks, grace periods, and recovery systems. Detail the streak visualization components, progress indicators, and streak-related achievements. Include examples of streak data models, date-based calculations, and integration with attendance records. Explain streak bonus systems, milestone celebrations, and psychological impact on user retention. Address streak reset scenarios, data synchronization across devices, and streak statistics reporting.

wiki_catalogs.name: Media Upload System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the media upload system. Detail the file upload workflow including drag-and-drop functionality, file type validation, and size restrictions. Document the supported media formats (images, videos, audio, PDFs) with their respective limitations and processing requirements. Explain the upload progress tracking, error handling, and retry mechanisms. Include the integration with Firebase Storage for media file management and the database updates for content references. Document the admin-only upload permissions, moderation workflows, and content approval processes. Address security measures including file scanning, virus detection, and access control.

wiki_catalogs.name: Access Control & Permissions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the payment-based access control system. Document how payment status determines user access permissions, subscription validation, and feature availability. Explain the integration between payment processing and user role management, including automatic permission updates based on payment status. Detail the access control implementation in the frontend, backend validation, and real-time permission synchronization. Include examples of access scenarios, permission checking mechanisms, and fallback strategies for unpaid users. Document the security measures for preventing unauthorized access and the audit trail for access control decisions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Schema Evolution & Migration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for database schema evolution and migration strategies. Detail the migration process for adding new fields, changing data types, and restructuring collections. Document the runAccessMigration function and similar migration utilities. Explain backward compatibility considerations, data transformation patterns, and rollback strategies. Include practical examples of common migration scenarios like adding new achievement types, updating user roles, or modifying course structures. Address testing migration scripts, production deployment strategies, and monitoring migration success. Provide guidelines for planning major schema changes and minimizing downtime during updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Reporting Systems
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the administrative reporting systems. Explain student performance analytics, financial reporting capabilities, and system usage metrics. Document the report generation interface, data visualization components, and export functionality. Detail the integration with gamification data for achievement tracking, attendance analytics, and financial data processing. Include practical examples of report creation workflows, data filtering options, and administrative reporting procedures for monitoring platform performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Modal & Dialog Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Modal component system. Document the modal's overlay behavior, backdrop interaction, and keyboard navigation support. Explain the component's props for controlling visibility, animation states, and content structure. Include examples of modal usage patterns including confirmation dialogs, form modals, and informational overlays. Detail accessibility features such as focus trapping, ARIA attributes, and screen reader support. Cover animation transitions, z-index management, and responsive behavior. Provide guidelines for modal composition, content organization, and integration with form handling patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for Firebase Authentication integration and custom claims management. Document the authentication flow including email/password, Google OAuth, and token verification processes. Detail custom claims implementation for role-based access control with admin privileges and student permissions. Document token handling, session management, and authentication state persistence. Include examples of protected route implementation, role-based UI rendering, and authentication guards. Address security considerations, token refresh mechanisms, and error handling for authentication failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Access Control
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive authentication and access control documentation for the Fluentoria application. Detail the Firebase authentication integration using onAuthStateChanged for real-time authentication monitoring, user role detection and management, and access control validation. Document the authentication flow from initial login through role assignment, payment status checking, and access authorization. Explain the admin email detection system, user role enforcement, and the blocking mechanism for unauthorized users. Include the integration with Firestore for user data retrieval, the authentication state persistence across page reloads, and the logout handling. Address the security considerations, error handling during authentication failures, and the seamless transition between authenticated and unauthenticated states.

wiki_catalogs.name: Utility Functions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create documentation for utility functions used across Firebase Cloud Functions. Document the ensureAdminContext function for context-based admin validation, ensureAdminFromRequest for Bearer token authentication, and runLegacyMigrationAsAdmin for comprehensive data migration. Detail the parseCourseIdFromExternalReference function with support for JSON payloads, prefixed formats, and backward compatibility. Explain the timing-safe comparison implementation using crypto.timingSafeEqual, legacy migration algorithms, and course ID extraction patterns. Document function parameters, return values, error conditions, and integration patterns with other Firebase functions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Subscriptions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document real-time data subscription patterns and implementation in Fluentoria. Explain Firestore listener management, subscription lifecycle, and cleanup strategies. Detail subscription patterns for courses, completions, user data, and administrative updates. Include examples of reactive data patterns, state synchronization, and performance optimization for real-time updates. Address subscription memory management, error handling, and connection resilience patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Course Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the course management system. Explain the course structure and organization including course creation, editing, and deletion workflows. Document the content types supported (video lessons, audio materials, PDF resources, interactive modules) and their management through the CourseForm component. Detail the course catalog architecture, search and filtering capabilities, and content delivery mechanisms. Include administrative features for course approval, scheduling, and access control. Explain the relationship between courses, modules, lessons, and student progress tracking. Provide practical examples of course creation workflows, content upload processes, and student enrollment management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Progress Visualization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for progress visualization components. Explain the progress bar implementation, level progress displays, and achievement completion tracking visuals. Document the UI components for showing overall progress, course completion percentages, and skill mastery indicators. Detail the visual design patterns, color coding systems, and progress animation effects. Include examples of progress data binding, real-time updates, and responsive design considerations. Explain the integration between different progress metrics, dashboard widgets, and user feedback mechanisms. Address accessibility features, progress export capabilities, and progress sharing functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Progress Tracking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the content progress tracking system. Document the lesson completion tracking including automatic progress detection, manual completion marking, and streak maintenance. Explain the integration with the gamification system for XP rewards and achievement unlocking upon content completion. Detail the attendance logging functionality that records user engagement with learning materials. Include the progress visualization components showing completion percentages, time spent, and learning milestones. Document the data persistence for progress records, completion timestamps, and user activity analytics. Address the relationship between content consumption and overall course completion metrics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Financial Reporting & Analytics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the financial reporting and analytics system. Document the data collection mechanisms for payment tracking, revenue analytics, and subscription metrics. Explain the integration with Asaas reporting APIs, data aggregation processes, and report generation workflows. Detail the financial data models, calculation methods for revenue tracking, and subscription analytics. Include examples of report generation, data visualization, and export capabilities. Document the security measures for financial data protection, audit trails, and compliance requirements. Address performance considerations for large-scale financial data processing and real-time reporting capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Settings & Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the settings and configuration management system. Explain system-wide configuration options, user preferences management, and administrative settings interfaces. Document the configuration data model, settings persistence mechanisms, and real-time configuration updates. Detail the integration with Firebase for settings storage, configuration validation, and access control for settings modifications. Include practical examples of configuration workflows, settings backup procedures, and administrative system customization options.

wiki_catalogs.name: Toggle & Switch Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Toggle component system. Document the component's switch behavior, state management, and visual feedback patterns. Explain the component's props for controlling initial state, disabled states, and custom styling. Include usage examples for different toggle configurations, form integration patterns, and accessibility considerations. Detail the animation effects during state changes, focus management, and keyboard interaction support. Cover the component's integration with form libraries, validation patterns, and real-time state updates. Provide guidelines for toggle customization, styling consistency, and responsive design approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Responsive Design & Layout System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed responsive design documentation for the Fluentoria application layout system. Document the adaptive layout architecture using Tailwind CSS with responsive breakpoints for mobile, tablet, and desktop views. Explain the fixed sidebar implementation on desktop with dynamic width adjustment, the mobile navigation adaptation with bottom navigation, and the overlay background system with gradient effects. Detail the responsive typography scaling, component sizing strategies, and the z-index layering system for proper stacking context. Include the PWA-specific considerations, touch interaction handling, and the responsive image loading strategies. Address the performance implications of responsive design, CSS optimization techniques, and the integration between desktop and mobile navigation patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Learning Content Delivery
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the learning content delivery system. Detail the video lesson integration with YouTube API, audio material playback functionality, and PDF resource management. Document the content progress tracking system, completion criteria, and student engagement metrics. Explain the media upload system including file type support, size limitations, and processing workflows. Include the integration with mindful flow content, music library management, and gallery content systems. Provide technical details on content caching, streaming optimization, and offline access capabilities. Address content moderation, quality assurance, and accessibility compliance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Gamification & Progress Tracking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the gamification and progress tracking system. Explain the achievement system design including achievement types, unlocking criteria, and reward mechanisms. Document the XP reward calculation, level progression mechanics, and streak tracking functionality. Detail the leaderboard implementation with real-time ranking updates and social comparison features. Include the habit formation monitoring system, daily practice integration, and progress visualization components. Explain the relationship between learning activities and gamification rewards, including behavioral psychology principles applied in the system. Provide examples of achievement configurations, progress analytics, and user motivation strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payment & Subscription Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the payment and subscription management system. Document the Asaas payment integration including customer creation, payment processing, and subscription management workflows. Detail the webhook handling for payment status updates, subscription renewal processes, and access control based on payment status. Include the Netlify Functions implementation for serverless payment processing, error handling strategies, and retry mechanisms. Explain the integration between payment status and user access permissions, financial reporting capabilities, and refund processing. Provide technical details on API endpoints, request/response schemas, authentication methods, and security considerations for payment data handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Administrative Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the administrative features and dashboard system. Explain the admin dashboard overview, student management capabilities including enrollment tracking and progress monitoring. Document the course administration tools, system configuration options, and access control management. Detail the reporting systems including student performance analytics, financial reports, and system usage metrics. Include the settings management interface, system maintenance tools, and user administration workflows. Explain the integration between administrative actions and real-time system updates, audit logging, and change tracking. Provide practical examples of administrative workflows and system management procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the state management architecture using Zustand. Detail the global state management implementation, store patterns, and component state coordination strategies. Document the custom hooks for data fetching and state management, including useCatalogData and useCatalogFilters. Explain the state update patterns, performance optimization techniques, and memory management strategies. Include the integration between global state and component local state, state persistence mechanisms, and debugging approaches. Provide practical examples of state management patterns, best practices for store organization, and performance considerations for large-scale applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Design & Abstraction
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the Firestore database schema and abstraction layer. Detail entity relationships including users, courses, lessons, achievements, and progress tracking. Document field definitions, data types, and validation rules across all collections. Explain the database query optimization strategies, indexing patterns, and performance considerations. Include the security rules implementation for data access control, user permissions, and data protection. Document the data migration strategies, schema evolution patterns, and backup procedures. Address data lifecycle management, retention policies, and archival strategies. Provide practical examples of common queries, data access patterns, and integration with Firebase services.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Component Library
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the reusable UI component library. Describe the component architecture, design patterns, and consistency standards across all UI components. Document each component's props, attributes, events, and customization options with practical usage examples. Include guidelines for responsive design, accessibility compliance, and cross-browser compatibility. Detail the styling strategy using Tailwind CSS, theme customization options, and component composition patterns. Explain the animation and transition systems, state management within components, and integration with the overall design system. Provide guidelines for component development, testing approaches, and maintenance procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Navigation & Routing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the navigation and routing system. Detail the routing architecture using React Router, route guards for authentication and authorization, and dynamic route generation. Document the navigation components including Sidebar and MobileNav, their responsive behavior, and role-based visibility. Explain the URL structure, navigation patterns, and breadcrumb systems. Include the integration between routing and state management, navigation persistence, and deep linking capabilities. Address mobile-first navigation design, keyboard navigation support, and accessibility considerations. Provide examples of custom route guards, navigation animations, and user experience optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Chat & Communication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the chat and communication system. Explain the real-time messaging implementation using Firebase Firestore, message persistence, and delivery confirmation mechanisms. Document the course discussion system, instructor-student interaction patterns, and group communication features. Detail the message types, formatting options, and attachment support. Include the integration with user authentication, message threading, and notification systems. Explain the real-time updates, offline message queuing, and synchronization strategies. Address security considerations for message content, moderation capabilities, and privacy controls. Provide examples of chat integration patterns and user interaction workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the testing strategy and implementation. Detail the Vitest configuration, unit testing patterns, and component testing approaches. Document the mock strategies for Firebase services, database interactions, and external API integrations. Explain the test setup procedures, environment configuration, and continuous integration practices. Include the testing patterns for async operations, state management testing, and UI component testing. Address performance testing, accessibility testing, and cross-browser compatibility testing. Provide guidelines for writing effective tests, test organization, and maintaining test suites. Include examples of common testing scenarios and debugging test failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build & Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for build configuration and deployment processes. Detail the Vite build configuration, environment variable management, and optimization strategies. Document the Tailwind CSS configuration, PostCSS processing, and asset optimization. Explain the deployment strategies for Firebase Hosting, Netlify Functions, and static site deployment. Include the Progressive Web App (PWA) configuration, service worker implementation, and offline capabilities. Detail the CI/CD pipeline setup, automated testing integration, and release management procedures. Address performance optimization, bundle analysis, and deployment troubleshooting. Provide examples of build customization, environment-specific configurations, and deployment verification procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines and best practices for contributing to Fluentoria. Detail the code style standards, TypeScript configuration, and component development patterns. Document the state management best practices, performance optimization guidelines, and architectural decision-making principles. Explain the component composition patterns, reusability guidelines, and design system adherence. Include the testing requirements, code review processes, and documentation standards. Address the development workflow, branch management, and contribution procedures. Provide guidelines for debugging, performance profiling, and troubleshooting common issues. Include examples of well-structured components, efficient state management, and maintainable code patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for all serverless functions and database interfaces. Document Firebase Cloud Functions including HTTP triggers, scheduled functions, and database triggers with request/response schemas and authentication methods. Detail the Netlify Functions API endpoints for payment processing, customer management, and webhook handling. Include the database query API with Firestore collection interfaces, security rules, and data access patterns. Document the Firebase Authentication API integration, custom claims management, and token handling. Provide practical examples of API usage, error handling strategies, rate limiting considerations, and security best practices. Address API versioning, backward compatibility, and migration procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting & FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting documentation and frequently asked questions for Fluentoria. Document common issues and their solutions including build errors, Firebase configuration problems, authentication failures, and deployment issues. Detail performance optimization strategies, debugging techniques, and error handling patterns. Include the error boundary implementation, error reporting mechanisms, and user-friendly error messaging. Address browser compatibility issues, mobile device troubleshooting, and network connectivity problems. Provide step-by-step diagnostic procedures, log analysis techniques, and recovery strategies. Include FAQ sections covering common user questions, development environment setup issues, and integration problems with external services.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Fluentoria: Comprehensive Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
Fluentoria is a sophisticated Language Learning Management System (LMS) designed to provide an immersive, gamified learning experience for English language students. The platform combines traditional course materials with modern digital tools, creating a comprehensive educational ecosystem that emphasizes habit formation over rote memorization.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Habit-Based Learning**: Transform language acquisition from knowledge-based study to habit-based practice
- **Gamified Experience**: Implement achievement systems, progress tracking, and reward mechanisms
- **Multi-Modal Content Delivery**: Support video lessons, audio materials, PDF resources, and mindful practices
- **Administrative Control**: Provide robust admin tools for course management and student oversight
- **Integrated Payment System**: Seamless subscription management through Asaas payment processing

### Target Audience
- **Primary**: Adult learners seeking practical English proficiency through daily habit formation
- **Secondary**: Educational institutions and language schools requiring comprehensive LMS capabilities
- **Administrators**: Educators and content managers overseeing course catalogs and student progress

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown
The application follows a modern React architecture with clear separation of concerns:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[App.tsx] --> B[Auth System]
    A --> C[Navigation System]
    A --> D[Content Management]
    
    B --> E[User Authentication]
    B --> F[Role Management]
    
    C --> G[Student Dashboard]
    C --> H[Admin Interface]
    
    D --> I[Course Catalog]
    D --> J[Media Library]
    D --> K[Gamification System]
    
    I --> L[Video Lessons]
    I --> M[Audio Materials]
    I --> N[PDF Resources]
    
    J --> O[Mindful Flow]
    J --> P[Music Library]
```

### Design Patterns
- **Store Pattern**: Centralized state management using Zustand for predictable state updates
- **Lazy Loading**: Code-splitting for optimal performance and reduced bundle size
- **Provider Pattern**: Context-based data sharing across component hierarchies
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic component rendering based on user roles and permissions

### System Relationships
The architecture implements a multi-layered approach with clear data flow:

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant A as App Component
    participant S as Store Layer
    participant F as Firebase
    participant DB as Firestore
    
    U->>A: Login Request
    A->>F: Authenticate User
    F->>DB: Verify Credentials
    DB-->>F: User Data
    F-->>A: Authenticated User
    A->>S: Update State
    S->>A: Render Dashboard
```

### Data Flow Diagrams
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Interaction] --> B[Event Handler]
    B --> C[Zustand Store]
    C --> D[Firebase Auth]
    D --> E[Firestore Database]
    E --> F[Real-time Updates]
    F --> G[Component Re-render]
    G --> H[UI Update]
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Application Root**: `App.tsx` - Central component managing authentication, navigation, and screen rendering
- **Entry Point**: `index.tsx` - React DOM rendering and service worker initialization
- **Type Definitions**: `types.ts` - Comprehensive TypeScript interfaces for all data structures

### Core Modules
- **State Management**: `lib/stores/appStore.ts` - Centralized application state using Zustand
- **Firebase Integration**: `lib/firebase.ts` - Firebase configuration and service initialization
- **Database Abstraction**: `lib/db/index.ts` - Barrel export for all database operations
- **UI Components**: `components/ui/` - Reusable component library with consistent styling

### Configuration Approach
- **Build Configuration**: `vite.config.ts` - Vite build system with React plugin and environment variable handling
- **Styling**: `tailwind.config.js` - Custom Tailwind CSS configuration with brand-specific color palette
- **Environment Variables**: `.env` files for Firebase and Asaas API credentials

### External Dependencies
Key dependencies include:
- **React 19.2.0**: Modern React features with concurrent rendering
- **Firebase 12.6.0**: Authentication, Firestore, and Cloud Functions
- **Zustand 5.0.11**: Lightweight state management solution
- **Tailwind CSS 4.1.17**: Utility-first CSS framework
- **Lucide React**: Consistent iconography across the application

### Integration Points
- **Asaas Payments**: `netlify/functions/` - Serverless functions for payment processing
- **Google Firebase**: Authentication, database, and cloud functions
- **YouTube Integration**: Video thumbnail generation and playback
- **PWA Support**: Service worker for offline functionality and app-like behavior

### Component Relationship Diagrams
```mermaid
graph LR
    A[App Component] --> B[Auth System]
    A --> C[Navigation]
    A --> D[Content Areas]
    
    B --> E[User Store]
    B --> F[Role Management]
    
    C --> G[Student Views]
    C --> H[Admin Views]
    
    D --> I[Course Content]
    D --> J[Media Library]
    D --> K[Gamification]
    
    I --> L[Video Player]
    I --> M[PDF Viewer]
    I --> N[Audio Player]
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview
Fluentoria provides a comprehensive educational platform with the following core features:

#### Learning Management
- **Course Catalog**: Organized lesson delivery with progress tracking
- **Multi-Format Content**: Video, audio, PDF, and interactive materials
- **Progress Monitoring**: Real-time completion tracking and statistics
- **Daily Practice**: Structured habit formation through consistent practice

#### Administrative Capabilities
- **User Management**: Student enrollment, progress monitoring, and access control
- **Course Administration**: Content creation, modification, and deletion
- **Financial Reporting**: Subscription management and revenue tracking
- **System Analytics**: Usage metrics and performance insights

#### Gamification System
- **Achievement Tracking**: Badge system for completed milestones
- **Level Progression**: Experience-based skill advancement
- **Leaderboards**: Social comparison and motivation
- **Reward Mechanisms**: Incentives for consistent practice

### Implementation Highlights
- **Authentication Flow**: `components/Auth.tsx` - Secure user login with Firebase
- **Course Management**: `components/CourseList.tsx` - Dynamic course discovery and selection
- **Payment Integration**: `components/AsaasPayment.tsx` - Seamless subscription processing
- **Progress Tracking**: `components/AttendanceTracker.tsx` - Habit formation monitoring

### Feature Architecture Diagrams
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Unauthenticated
    Unauthenticated --> Authenticating: Login Attempt
    Authenticating --> Student: Successful Auth
    Authenticating --> Admin: Admin Credentials
    Authenticating --> Blocked: Payment Pending
    
    Student --> Dashboard: Navigate
    Student --> CourseSelection: Select Course
    Student --> Profile: View Details
    
    Admin --> Reports: Access Dashboard
    Admin --> StudentManagement: Manage Users
    Admin --> CourseManagement: Edit Content
    
    Blocked --> PaymentProcessing: Process Payment
    PaymentProcessing --> Student: Access Granted
    PaymentProcessing --> Blocked: Payment Failed
    
    Dashboard --> CourseSelection: Continue Learning
    CourseSelection --> LessonView: Start Lesson
    LessonView --> Dashboard: Complete Lesson
```

### Performance Considerations
- **Code Splitting**: Lazy loading of route components for optimal loading times
- **State Optimization**: Efficient store management to minimize unnecessary re-renders
- **Caching Strategy**: Local cache for offline access and improved performance
- **Responsive Design**: Mobile-first approach with adaptive layouts

## 5. Conclusion and Recommendations

### Project Architecture Summary
Fluentoria demonstrates a well-structured, scalable educational platform that successfully combines modern React development practices with comprehensive learning management capabilities. The architecture effectively separates concerns through clear component hierarchies, implements robust state management, and integrates multiple third-party services seamlessly.

### Architectural Strengths
- **Modular Design**: Clear separation between UI components, business logic, and data layers
- **Scalable State Management**: Zustand provides efficient state handling without excessive complexity
- **Performance Optimization**: Code splitting and lazy loading ensure optimal user experience
- **Security Implementation**: Firebase authentication and secure API integrations
- **Extensible Framework**: Well-defined interfaces support future feature additions

### Potential Improvement Areas
- **Testing Coverage**: Expand unit and integration testing beyond current scope
- **Error Handling**: Implement more granular error boundaries and user feedback
- **Accessibility**: Enhance ARIA labels and keyboard navigation support
- **Documentation**: Add comprehensive API documentation and developer guides
- **Monitoring**: Implement application performance monitoring and error tracking

### Actionable Recommendations
1. **Implement Comprehensive Testing**: Add Vitest configurations for all major components and store logic
2. **Enhance Error Boundaries**: Create more specific error handling for different failure scenarios
3. **Optimize Performance**: Implement React Profiler integration and bundle analysis
4. **Expand Documentation**: Create developer documentation for API endpoints and component usage
5. **Security Hardening**: Add input validation and sanitization for all user-generated content

### Next Steps for Project Evolution
- **Mobile App Development**: Consider React Native implementation for native mobile experience
- **AI Integration**: Explore OpenAI integration for personalized learning recommendations
- **Advanced Analytics**: Implement detailed learning analytics and progress prediction
- **Multi-Language Support**: Add internationalization for broader market reach
- **Offline Mode Enhancement**: Improve service worker capabilities for better offline functionality

Sources:
- [package.json](package.json)
- [App.tsx](App.tsx)
- [types.ts](types.ts)
- [lib/stores/appStore.ts](lib/stores/appStore.ts)
- [lib/firebase.ts](lib/firebase.ts)
- [lib/db/index.ts](lib/db/index.ts)
- [vite.config.ts](vite.config.ts)
- [tailwind.config.js](tailwind.config.js)
- [components/StudentDashboard.tsx](components/StudentDashboard.tsx)
- [components/CourseList.tsx](components/CourseList.tsx)
- [lib/db/config.ts](lib/db/config.ts)
- [netlify/functions/check-payment-status.js](netlify/functions/check-payment-status.js)
- [index.tsx](index.tsx)

</blog>
